title: What an American thinks about Peru

transcript.text: Hello what an american thinks about peru um I was there for like three months one thing I noticed
About peru is that the little kids they were really outgoing I was sitting in a bus station
And some little girl was like hey what's your deal where are you going what you where you got what
And I was like hey what's up like telling them um there's lots of vegetarianism in peru peru is a
Cool place man peru is like my favorite country I've heard a lot of people go there like get
Mugged and stuff I didn't experience any of that but peruvians are like the most advanced I think
Culturally the most advanced in south america they're I mean they were really into they're poor
But they were really into vegetarianism everywhere you go there was a vegetarian
Uh like restaurant you know and they're they're and like juicing and stuff and then they had the
Alien guys people they were into aliens over there and the hindu mahaburata and stuff like regular poor
Peruvians you meet and it had a cool like magical feel to it the whole place cuzco is a total um
Um uh tourist area but um yeah machu picchu is beautiful amazing and our tourist tour guide took
It really really uh seriously lima the capital is really big and it's really polluted with uh guara
Which is uh fog really thick fog like crazy thick fog um yeah wild
And really good surfing but it's really dirty I could see the foam was brown and I guess that's
From vcs but the surfing there was incredible once they clean up that water out there that surfing is
Going to be unreal and they were doing um I mean the whole way along the way there's just different
Waves waves wave after wave yeah and they're also doing um
Uh paragliding you can paraglide off of the cliff um that's right next to the sea the sea comes and
Then there's the beach and then there's these big cliffs so maybe that's why they made it the capital
Because it was really well fortified but people were paragliding off of that onto the beach
Um the capital has two central uh plazas which is interesting usually I only have one plaza
And a big city the big town or a big like hill next to it and you can't only see like two blocks
From the top of the hill because of the guara that fog um yeah it's a big city but I didn't really
Experience that much of it you can't really see it because it's all the fog but I could see the poor
Neighborhoods there right next to the downtown but it's surrounded by desert like no trees it's in
The middle of the desert so it's interesting but peru has a lot of um a lot of um archaeology
They've got these uh this place called I can't in trujillo I can't remember what it's called
But this really ancient civilization there and it was a huge fortress you know surrounded by a big wall
And then these two big pyramids that were nearby and um and uh yeah but peru has it all they have
The deserts like up north and then they have these the huge mountains and and huaraz was beautiful
Huaraz uh this valley surrounded by these huge mountains oh man I'm starting to like want to go
Back and the peruvians are they're really friendly to me they don't like to get photographed
But yeah it was a really pleasant safe feeling place yeah and then they've got
Eka where they found the alien heads that's where I met some alien dude or dude who liked the aliens
He had these sitting on the street with these pamphlets like trying to tell you about the aliens
And uh yeah they have these big sand dunes there and then south of that was
The nazca lines that place is jam-packed and they've got the lake oh my yeah um
Okay so that's what america thinks about peru
